data.title: WhatConverts: Track Every Lead and Know What Works
data.excerpt: Discover how WhatConverts reveals which marketing campaigns drive real leads through calls, forms, and chats. Try it today.

$media.alt: WhatConverts: Track Every Lead and Know What Works

children.text: You spend thousands on ads, SEO, and social campaigns. But when a new client calls your office, do you actually know which channel brought them in? Most businesses guess. WhatConverts removes the guessing entirely.

This marketing attribution platform tracks every lead—phone calls, form submissions, and live chats—and ties each one back to the exact campaign, keyword, or ad that generated it. No more wondering if your Google Ads budget is working. No more debating whether that Facebook campaign is worth the spend. WhatConverts gives you proof.
I've spent weeks testing the platform, and I genuinely believe it fills a gap most businesses don't realize they have. Here's everything you need to know.
See WhatConverts pricing and plans here
What Makes WhatConverts Different From Other Analytics Tools
Plenty of tools track website visits. Google Analytics tells you how many people landed on your site. WhatConverts goes deeper—it tells you who those people are and what they actually did.

When someone calls your business after clicking a Google ad, WhatConverts records that call, captures the caller's details, tags it to the specific keyword and campaign, and logs it in your dashboard. You can even listen to call recordings to qualify the lead without lifting a finger.

Form submissions get the same treatment. Every field, every detail, every source attribution—all organized in one place. And for businesses using live chat, WhatConverts captures those conversations too, pulling chat transcripts into your lead report automatically.

The real magic happens in the reporting. You can filter leads by source, medium, campaign, keyword, landing page, or even geography. Want to know which Facebook ad group drives phone calls from Texas? Two clicks. Curious which organic keywords generate form fills? Done.
This level of detail turns marketing from a cost center into a predictable revenue engine. You stop spending blindly and start investing in what actually produces customers.
Who Is WhatConverts Ideal For?
WhatConverts works exceptionally well for specific types of businesses and professionals:
Agencies managing client campaigns.

If you run ads or SEO for clients, proving ROI is your biggest challenge. WhatConverts lets you show clients exactly how many leads each campaign generated, complete with call recordings and form data. Client retention goes up when you can point to real numbers.
Local and service-based businesses.

Plumbers, law firms, dental practices, home renovation companies—any business where a phone call or form submission is the primary conversion event. These businesses often struggle to attribute inbound calls to specific campaigns, and WhatConverts solves that directly.
E-commerce brands with phone orders.
If your customers sometimes call to place orders or ask questions before buying online, you need to know which ads drove those calls. Standard analytics tools miss these conversions entirely.
Marketing consultants and freelancers.
When you need to justify your work to clients or stakeholders, having a clean dashboard that shows "this campaign produced 47 qualified leads this month" is incredibly powerful.
Multi-location businesses.
WhatConverts supports tracking across multiple locations, phone numbers, and even different websites. Each location gets its own attribution data while everything rolls up into a master view.
If your business depends on inbound leads through any combination of phone, forms, or chat, this platform was built for you.
Start tracking your real leads with WhatConverts
Standout Features Worth Highlighting
WhatConverts has rolled out several features that make it stand out in the attribution space:
Dynamic Number Insertion (DNI).

This swaps phone numbers on your website based on the visitor's traffic source. A visitor from Google Ads sees a different number than someone from organic search. Every call gets tagged to its source automatically—no manual work required.
Lead Intelligence.

Beyond basic tracking, WhatConverts enriches lead data with information about the caller or form submitter. You see the lead's name, company, location, and interaction history without asking them to fill out extra fields.
Quotable Leads.

Not all leads are equal. WhatConverts lets you mark leads as quotable (meaning they represent a real sales opportunity) versus spam or duplicates. This gives you a much more accurate picture of actual ROI versus raw lead counts.
Google Ads and Analytics integrations.

WhatConverts pushes conversion data directly into Google Ads, letting you optimize campaigns based on actual lead quality rather than just form fills or page views. This is a game-changer for anyone running paid search.
Custom reporting and dashboards.

Build reports that match how your business actually thinks about performance. Filter by date range, campaign, source, lead type, or any custom field. Export everything to PDF or share live dashboards with stakeholders.
Call recordings and transcripts.

Every tracked call comes with a recording. You can listen back, review conversation quality, and use real calls to train your sales team. Some users report that call review alone is worth the subscription cost.
Multi-channel attribution in one view.
Calls, forms, and chats all live in a single unified dashboard. No juggling three different tools. No exporting CSVs and merging spreadsheets. Everything flows into one clean interface.
For a deeper dive into getting started, check out our
step-by-step guide on how to use WhatConverts
How WhatConverts Compares to What You're Probably Using Now

If you're currently relying on Google Analytics alone for conversion tracking, you're only seeing half the picture. Google Analytics counts sessions and page views beautifully, but it doesn't tell you that the person who called your office came from a specific Instagram ad. It doesn't record that phone call or capture the caller's details.

WhatConverts fills that blind spot completely. It sits alongside your existing analytics stack and adds the attribution layer that most tools miss. You don't have to replace anything—you just add WhatConverts to get the full story.

Compared to standalone call tracking tools, WhatConverts bundles call tracking, form tracking, and chat tracking into a single platform with unified reporting. Instead of paying for three separate subscriptions and trying to stitch the data together manually, you get everything in one dashboard.

The 4.9 out of 5 rating on G2 from over 300 reviews tells you something important: users don't just tolerate this tool—they genuinely love it. That kind of satisfaction score is rare in the analytics space, where most tools hover around 4.2 to 4.5.
WhatConverts also integrates smoothly with popular project management and CRM tools. If your team uses platforms like
ClickUp
or
monday.com
for workflow management, you can pipe lead data into your existing processes without friction.
Real-World Use Cases That Prove the Value
Let me walk through a few scenarios where WhatConverts transforms how a business operates:
Scenario 1: PPC agency proving ROI.

An agency managing Google Ads for a roofing company uses WhatConverts to track every phone call generated by paid campaigns. At the end of each month, they produce a report showing 83 qualified calls, 12 of which converted to signed contracts worth $156,000 in revenue. The client never questions the management fee again.
Scenario 2: Law firm optimizing ad spend.

A personal injury attorney runs ads across Google, Facebook, and Bing. Without WhatConverts, they only tracked form submissions and assumed calls were organic. After implementing WhatConverts, they discovered 60% of their phone leads came from a single Bing Ads campaign they almost cancelled. That campaign became their most profitable channel.
Scenario 3: SaaS company with sales calls.

A B2B software company uses WhatConverts to track demo requests through both forms and phone calls. They discover that blog content drives 3x more demo calls than paid social, shifting their content strategy and reducing customer acquisition cost by 40%.
Want more examples? Our
WhatConverts use cases guide
covers dozens of industries and scenarios.
Practical Tips for Getting Maximum Value
After spending significant time with the platform, here are my top recommendations:
Set up DNI immediately.

Dynamic number insertion is the foundation of everything WhatConverts does. Without it, you're leaving call attribution on the table. The setup takes about 15 minutes and pays dividends every single day.
Use the quotable lead feature consistently.

Train your team to mark leads as quotable or not quotable within 24 hours. This keeps your data clean and makes your reports dramatically more useful. Raw lead counts are misleading; quotable lead counts tell the real story.
Review call recordings weekly.

Set aside 30 minutes each week to listen to a sample of recorded calls. You'll uncover sales training opportunities, discover which campaigns drive high-intent callers, and catch quality issues before they cost you customers.
Connect Google Ads conversion tracking.

Pushing WhatConverts data into Google Ads lets the algorithm optimize for actual qualified leads, not just clicks or form fills. This single integration often improves campaign performance by 20-30% within the first month.
Build custom dashboards for each stakeholder.

Your CEO wants high-level ROI numbers. Your marketing manager wants keyword-level detail. Your sales team wants a list of today's new leads. WhatConverts lets you create tailored views for each audience without exporting data.
For more optimization strategies, check out our
WhatConverts tips and best practices
. And if you want to explore what's new, our guide to
WhatConverts new features
covers the latest updates.
Pricing and Value

WhatConverts offers tiered pricing based on the number of tracked leads and features you need. Plans start affordably for small businesses and scale up for agencies managing dozens of clients. Every plan includes call tracking, form tracking, and reporting—no essential features locked behind enterprise paywalls.

Given that a single new customer is often worth hundreds or thousands of dollars, the subscription pays for itself after just a handful of attributed leads. Most users report positive ROI within the first week of proper setup.
Check current WhatConverts pricing and start your trial
The Bottom Line

WhatConverts solves a problem that almost every business has but few address properly: knowing exactly which marketing efforts generate real customers. It tracks calls, forms, and chats with precision, presents the data in clean dashboards, and integrates with the tools you already use.
The G2 reviews speak for themselves—4.9 stars from 300+ verified users. The platform is reliable, the support team is responsive, and the feature set keeps expanding.

If you're tired of guessing which campaigns work and which ones waste money, WhatConverts gives you the answer. Install it, set up dynamic number insertion, and within 24 hours you'll have more attribution clarity than you've ever had.

complete WhatConverts overview

children.text: What types of leads does WhatConverts track?

WhatConverts tracks three primary lead types: phone calls (with recording and transcription), form submissions (capturing all field data), and live chat conversations. All three are unified in a single dashboard with full source attribution.
How does WhatConverts attribute phone calls to specific marketing campaigns?

WhatConverts uses Dynamic Number Insertion (DNI), which displays different phone numbers on your website depending on each visitor's traffic source. When someone calls that number, the platform automatically tags the call to the correct campaign, keyword, ad group, or channel.
Is WhatConverts suitable for agencies managing multiple clients?

Absolutely. WhatConverts supports multi-client account structures, making it easy for agencies to manage tracking for dozens of clients from a single login. You can generate per-client reports, set up separate dashboards, and demonstrate clear ROI for each account.
